task_completion.description: Log in to FreeMED as the admin user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeMED login screen with 'admin' entered, followed by the FreeMED dashboard indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the global system template or macro module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeMED interface shows a 'Templates', 'Macros', or similar administrative module is open. It must NOT be inside a specific patient's chart (e.g., no patient name or demographics should be active).

task_completion.description: Enter the template name 'Normal Physical Exam'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Normal Physical Exam' is typed into the title, name, or description field for the new template.

task_completion.description: Enter the first half of the template body, including general and respiratory findings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main text area contains the required template text, specifically including the phrases 'in no acute distress' and 'clear to auscultation bilaterally'.

task_completion.description: Enter the second half of the template body, including cardiovascular findings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main text area contains the required template text, specifically including the phrase 'No murmurs, rubs, or gallops'.

task_completion.description: Save the newly created global template.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks a 'Save', 'Add', or 'Submit' button, or a success message is displayed indicating the template has been successfully saved to the system.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeMED graphical user interface to create the template.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web browser and FreeMED UI, rather than terminal commands, curl requests, or direct database insertions.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual FreeMED application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The template is being created inside the FreeMED web application, not in a local text editor, notepad, or unrelated application.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely filled out and submitted the form.
integrity.visual_evidence: Text is visibly entered into the appropriate input fields and saved, rather than the agent using browser developer tools to manipulate the DOM or fake a success message.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts for the VLM verifier: The expected template title is exactly 'Normal Physical Exam' (worth 25 points). The template body must contain specific phrases, including 'clear to auscultation bilaterally', 'in no acute distress', and 'No murmurs, rubs, or gallops' (phrases are worth up to 50 points). The VLM must specifically verify that the agent used the system template module to create a global template, rather than adding a note to an individual patient's chart (worth 25 points).
